title: angelica archangelica var. rwegica
intro: Angelica archangelica var. rwegica, also known as wild angelica, is a variety of the angelica plant that grows in the wild and is native to Europe. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of wild angelica include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive problems and skin conditions. Wild angelica is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be used in salves and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
